text: When you write code, you will inevitably encounter bugs. Identifying bugs starts with understanding what the program should do versus what it actually does. A systematic approach is essential: first, reproduce the error consistently, then isolate the part of the code causing the problem. Use debugging tools like breakpoints in your IDE to step through the code line by line, inspecting variable values. Alternatively, logging is a powerful technique—insert print statements or use logging libraries to track how data changes during execution. By following a logical process and analyzing the logged output, you can locate and fix errors efficiently.
